name: Janice Okun's Buffalo Wings
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut off and discard the small tip of each wing.
2. Cut the main wing bone and second wing bone at the joint.
3. Sprinkle the wings with salt, if desired, and pepper to taste.
4. Heat the oil in a deep-fat fryer or large casserole until it reaches about 350°F (175°C).
5. Add half of the wings to the hot oil and cook for approximately 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until they are golden brown and crisp. Remove with tongs and drain on paper towels.
6. Repeat step 4 with the remaining wings, cooking them for another 10 minutes or until they are also golden brown and crisp. Drain again on paper towels.
7. Melt the butter in a saucepan over medium heat.
8. Add 2 to 5 tablespoons of hot sauce and vinegar to the melted butter. Stir well.
9. Arrange the cooked wings on a warm serving platter.
10. Pour the butter mixture evenly over the chicken wings.
11. Serve with blue cheese dressing and celery sticks.
